title: Java: Beginner to Mastery
subtitle: Theory, coding challenges, mini-IDE, and major projects in one course.

challenge.instructions: Print two lines: your goal for learning Java, then the phrase `Hello, JVM`.
Print the Java version string you expect to use locally (e.g., `JDK 21`) and print `javac` is the compiler.
hints: This is output-only practice; any clear strings are fine.

majorProjects.title: Project 0 — Learning journal CLI
majorProjects.description: Build a text-based menu program that lets you append dated notes to a file `journal.txt` (create if missing). Support: add note, list last 5 notes, exit. Parse input with `Scanner`.
stretchGoals: Refactor into small methods with clear names.

sections.title: Syntax fundamentals: types, control flow, arrays, methods

challenge.instructions: Print one line explaining bytecode in your own words (10+ words).
Declare a `byte`, a `long` literal, and a `double`. Print their sum as a `double`.
Given `int a = 17, b = 5`, print integer division and remainder on separate lines.
Print the numbers 1 through 20, but print `Fizz` for multiples of 3, `Buzz` for multiples of 5, `FizzBuzz` for both.
Compute the average of `{2, 4, 6, 8}` using a loop and print it as a `double`.
Write `static int max(int a, int b)` and print `max(9, 4)`.

majorProjects.title: Project 1 — Terminal calculator
majorProjects.description: Implement a REPL that reads lines like `3 + 4` or `10 / 2` (integer math is fine). Support `+ - * /` and `quit`. Handle divide-by-zero gracefully.
stretchGoals: Add parentheses parsing (shunting-yard or recursive descent).

sections.title: Object-oriented Java: classes, packages, static/final, records

challenge.instructions: Create a `BankAccount` class with `double balance`, `void deposit(double)`, `void withdraw(double)` (no negative balance). In `main`, print balance after a few operations.
Explain in one printed line why reverse-DNS package names reduce collisions.
Declare `static final double PI = 3.141592653589793` and print the circumference of a circle with radius 5.
Define `record Book(String title, int pages)` and print a `Book` instance's `toString()`.

majorProjects.title: Project 2 — Library catalog
majorProjects.description: Model `Book` and `Library` with add/list/search by title substring. Keep data in-memory. Provide a simple text menu.
stretchGoals: Persist to a CSV file on exit and reload on start.

challenge.instructions: Read no input: build the string `A-B-C-...-Z` using `StringBuilder` and print it.
Write `static int parsePositiveInt(String s)` that throws `IllegalArgumentException` for null, blank, or non-positive values. In `main`, catch and print a friendly message.
Add an `assert` that `Math.sqrt(x) >= 0` for `x = 2` (note: assertions may be disabled unless `-ea`). Print a line explaining assertions are for internal invariants.
Create `enum Direction { N, E, S, W }` and print all values using `values()`.

majorProjects.title: Project 3 — Robust CSV stats
majorProjects.description: Read a small embedded CSV string in code (no file required) representing `name,score`. Parse with `String.split`, convert scores safely, skip bad rows with clear error messages, print min/max/average.
stretchGoals: Read from a real file using `Files.readString` locally (IDE), keep embedded mode for the web runner.

challenge.instructions: Implement a simple `class Box<T>` with `void set(T v)` and `T get()`. Store a `String` and print it.
Create `ArrayList<Integer>`, add 3,1,4,1,5, remove all `1`s, print the list.
Sort `List<String>` `["bbb","a","cc"]` by length then lexicographically. Print the list.
Given `List.of(1,2,3,4,5)`, print the sum of squares of even numbers using a stream pipeline.

majorProjects.title: Project 4 — Inventory with maps
majorProjects.description: Track item SKU → quantity with `HashMap`. Support add/remove/adjust/print. Use `record Item(String sku, String name)` for metadata in another map if you like.
stretchGoals: Persist map to JSON manually (string concat) for fun—or use Jackson locally.

sections.title: Inheritance, abstract types, sealed types, nested classes

challenge.instructions: Model `Animal` with `String speak()` overridden by `Cat` and `Dog`. Print each speak.
Create interface `Drawable` with `String draw()`. Implement it for `Square`. Print `draw()`.

If your JDK supports it, write a tiny sealed interface `Shape` with `Circle` and `Rectangle` permits and print areas via `instanceof` pattern matching. If not supported in the runner, comment that and print a note.
Demonstrate a `static` nested class `Outer.Util` with a static method returning `int` sum.

majorProjects.title: Project 5 — Polymorphic simulation (tick-based)
majorProjects.description: Create an `interface Entity { void tick(World w); }` and 2–3 entities with simple rules on a small grid printed as text. Run 10 ticks.

challenge.instructions: Use `Predicate<String>` to test blank strings and print results for `{"a","", " "}`.
Group words by first letter using streams: input list `List.of("apple","apricot","banana")`, print map.
Write `static Optional<String> findShort(String s, int maxLen)` returning empty if null or longer than max. Demonstrate `orElse`.

majorProjects.title: Project 6 — Log query tool
majorProjects.description: Model `record LogLine(Instant ts, String level, String msg)` (use `Instant.now()` fake times if easier). Provide filtering by level, substring search, and counting by level using streams.

challenge.instructions: Create `@interface Todo { String value(); }` and annotate `main` method (retention runtime optional). Print a line explaining metadata use.
Use reflection to print the declared methods of `String.class` count (not listing all if too long—print count only).
Print 2 lines: what `exports` does in `module-info.java`, and what `requires` does.

majorProjects.title: Project 7 — Mini plugin loader (classpath simulation)
majorProjects.description: Define a `Greeter` interface and two implementations in the same file for simplicity. Select implementation name from `args[0]` via `switch` and print greeting (fake plugin loading without URLClassLoader).
stretchGoals: Actually load from separate `.java` files locally and compile with `javac` script.

sections.title: IO/NIO.2, serialization awareness, regex, time, i18n

challenge.instructions: Print a line recommending `Files.readString` for small text files and `BufferedInputStream` for large binary reads.
Print three bullet-like lines (plain text) describing risks of Java deserialization on untrusted data.
Extract all integers from `"a1b22c333"` using `Pattern`/`Matcher` and print their sum.
Print today's ISO local date using `LocalDate.now()` and the current instant using `Instant.now()`.
Print numbers `1234567.89` formatted for `Locale.US` and `Locale.GERMANY` using `NumberFormat`.

majorProjects.title: Project 8 — Time zone meeting planner (text)
majorProjects.description: Given two zones and a list of candidate `LocalTime` windows, print which candidates are workable (simulate with fixed offsets using `ZoneOffset.ofHours`).

challenge.instructions: Start two threads that each print 5 lines with their name (`Thread.currentThread().getName()`). Join both before exiting `main`.
Use `Executors.newFixedThreadPool(2)` to submit two `Callable<Integer>` tasks that sleep 100ms and return values; print sum of futures.
Print a careful explanation: why `volatile` alone may not fix `i++` races.
Demonstrate `ConcurrentHashMap.merge` to count word frequencies in a sentence.

majorProjects.title: Project 9 — Web crawler toy (single JVM)
majorProjects.description: No real network required: simulate downloading URLs with `Thread.sleep` random ms. Use a fixed pool to process 20 URLs and collect statistics (min/max simulated latency).

sections.title: Testing concepts, profiling, JVM memory/GC, classloading

challenge.instructions: Write `static void assertEven(int n)` throwing `IllegalStateException` if odd. Call it from `main` with try/catch to simulate a test failure message.
Print two lines contrasting stubs vs mocks in your own words.
Print why naive `System.nanoTime` loops can mislead without warmup.
Print a paragraph describing a scenario where creating many short-lived objects is OK due to generational GC (high-level).
Explain in printed lines what a `ClassLoader` does at a high level.
Print a checklist (5 items) for investigating a slow Java service before tuning JVM flags.

majorProjects.title: Project 10 — Deterministic simulation with property tests (manual)
majorProjects.description: Implement a pure `Money` class (cents as `long`) with add/subtract and test it from `main` using a batch of assertions (throw on failure). No JUnit required in the runner.
stretchGoals: Generate random cases in `main` with a fixed seed.

challenge.instructions: Print 5 secure coding practices relevant to Java servers.
Print an example log line including `level`, `thread`, `logger`, and `message` fields as JSON text.
Print a code sketch (as comments) showing `HttpClient.newHttpClient().send(...)` with timeout—no need to actually call network.

majorProjects.title: Project 11 — Audit log library (in-memory)
majorProjects.description: Implement `AuditLog` storing immutable events (`Instant`, `String user`, `String action`). Provide query by user and export as CSV string.
stretchGoals: Add ring buffer max size with drop policy.

challenge.instructions: Implement `interface PricingStrategy { int price(int cents); }` and two strategies (full price, 10% off). Print results for 1000 cents.
Pick one SOLID letter and print a Java-specific example scenario where it helps.
Print a layered diagram in ASCII (3 layers) for a hypothetical app: UI, domain, infrastructure.

majorProjects.title: Project 12 — Refactor spaghetti into layers
majorProjects.description: Start from a purposely messy `main` that mixes IO and domain rules. Extract pure domain methods and separate printing. Keep behavior identical.
stretchGoals: Add tests for domain methods locally.

sections.title: Build tools, CI, Spring orientation, JDBC, REST, microservices caution

challenge.instructions: Print what `mvn verify` typically runs, in your own words, in 2–3 lines.
List 6 CI steps as separate println lines for a Java library.
Explain dependency injection in 3 println lines: what, why, example.
Print why `PreparedStatement` is preferred over string concatenation for SQL values.
Print example HTTP request line + headers for `GET /users/42` with `Accept: application/json`.
Print two pros and two cons of microservices from a Java team perspective.

majorProjects.title: Project 13 — Spring Boot local service (recommended)
majorProjects.description: In IntelliJ, create a Spring Boot app with one `GET /hello` endpoint returning JSON `{message:"..."}`. This site cannot run Spring for you, but this is the bridge to industry skills.
stretchGoals: Add Spring Data JPA entity + repository + H2.
Add validation and global exception handler.

challenge.instructions: Print your personal plan: 3 bullet lines with topics you will deepen next based on this course.

majorProjects.title: CAPSTONE A — Budget tracker with reports
majorProjects.description: Categories, monthly totals, export CSV, and invariants (no negative balances unless credit). CLI or desktop optional.
stretchGoals: Charts in a local web UI.

majorProjects.title: CAPSTONE B — Multi-user chat server ( sockets )
majorProjects.description: Java `ServerSocket` accepts clients; broadcast messages; graceful shutdown; basic protocol (`JOIN`, `MSG`, `LEAVE`).

majorProjects.title: CAPSTONE C — Build your own test runner
majorProjects.description: Reflectively find `@Test` annotated methods (custom annotation) on classes you list, invoke, collect failures. This deepens reflection and classloading.

majorProjects.title: CAPSTONE D — Plugin-based rule engine
majorProjects.description: Define `Rule` interface, load rules from a config list, apply to a `Fact` map. Great for OOP + architecture practice.
